info.name: Pet Shop API
info.description:
End-to-end tester for the Pet Shop backend.

**Setup**
1. Import this collection.
2. Edit the collection variables — set `admin_email` and `admin_password` to match your backend `.env` (the seeded first admin). Optionally change `base_url`, `office_password`, `rep_password`.
3. Open the Collection Runner and run the **End-to-End Flow** folder in order. It performs a complete happy path: login → seed users → seed taxonomy → seed a product with option_sets → set opening stock → sales rep creates a store → office approves it → attendance → place order → office progresses through accept / pack / dispatch / deliver.
4. For ad-hoc testing, use the **Explore** folder — each request is independent, but any request that needs an ID reads it from a collection variable so run the corresponding End-to-End step first.

**CSRF is handled automatically.** A collection-level test hook reads the `petshop_csrf` cookie from every response and stores it as `{{csrf_token}}`. Every state-changing request references that variable in the `X-CSRF-Token` header.

**Cookies** are stored in Postman's per-domain cookie jar — the `Login as X` requests overwrite the session cookies each time you switch users.

exec: // After every response, if the server set a fresh CSRF cookie,
// capture it into a collection variable so subsequent writes can use it.

13. Find inventory row for this variant

20. Sales rep places an order (5 units)

item.name: Explore
item.description: Ad-hoc requests grouped by domain. IDs referenced here come from collection variables — run the matching End-to-End step first (or set the variable manually).

item.name: Delete category (refuses if it still has subcategories)
PATCH variant with reason (records to price_history if price changes)
Bulk upload (attach a .xlsx via Body -> form-data -> file)

request.description:
Accepted columns (case-insensitive):

Product-level (first row per product name wins):
  Product Name           (required, merge key)
  Client Product Code    (optional)
  Category               (required, must exist)
  Subcategory            (required, must exist)
  Description            (optional)
  Brand                  (optional)
  Unit                   (optional; kg, pcs, box, ...)
  Base Price             (required OR variant_price)
  Discount Price         (optional)
  Images                 (comma-separated URLs)

Variant-level (one row per variant):
  Variant Name           (display label, e.g. '1kg Combo Pack')
  Size / Weight / Color
  SKU
  Variant Image          (URL)
  Variant Price          (falls back to Base Price)
  Variant Discount Price
  Opening Stock          (writes to inventory)
  Reorder Level          (writes to inventory)

Max 5MB .xlsx or .xlsm.

item.name: Admin/office creates already-approved store for a rep

exec: pm.test('Status is approved (no separate approval step)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().status).to.eql('approved'));

item.name: In-store visit — no order (with check_in/check_out for duration)

body.raw:
{
  "store_id": "{{store_id}}",
  "mode": "in_store",
  "outcome": "no_order",
  "lat": {{store_lat}},
  "lng": {{store_lng}},
  "check_in":  "2026-07-19T10:00:00Z",
  "check_out": "2026-07-19T10:30:00Z",
  "no_order_reason": "Store closed for stock-taking; will revisit tomorrow",
  "remarks": "Left samples"
}
